root_cause: The public REST endpoint (rest.xialab.ca/api/mapcompounds) is a broken Payara Java EE servlet returning HTTP 500 for all inputs. The MetaboAnalyst R package actually uses a completely different internal endpoint (www.xialab.ca/api/internal_mapcompounds) that accepts R binary .rds multipart uploads and returns serialized R objects — not accessible from Python or curl. The public JSON API documented in tutorials has been broken since at least December 2024 with no fix from the team.
retry_notes: Both issues opened Dec 2024 / Dec 2025 with no maintainer response. Check if either is resolved before retrying.
workaround: Use KEGG REST API (rest.kegg.jp) for compound name resolution and pathway-metabolite mappings, combined with local scipy.stats.hypergeom for enrichment statistics and Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction. Implemented in src/tooluniverse/metaboanalyst_tool.py
investigation_notes: rest.xialab.ca/api/mapcompounds returns HTTP 500 for all inputs including official tutorial examples
www.xialab.ca/api/internal_mapcompounds returns HTTP 415 for JSON — requires multipart/form-data with R .rds binary file
R source code (.do.api.call) confirmed: POST body = list(rds = upload_file('tosend.rds', 'application/octet-stream')), response = unserialize(raw bytes)
The 'REST API' documented at rest.xialab.ca is a separate broken servlet; the working internal API requires R binary serialization
www.xialab.ca/api/login still returns 200 (session management works), but computation endpoints are broken

attempted_config.name: MetaboAnalyst_pathway_enrichment
attempted_config.description: Perform pathway enrichment analysis using MetaboAnalyst REST API
